steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content: The angles in a triangle sum to $180^{\circ}$. Since $\angle U=58^{\circ}$, the other two angles must sum to $122^{\circ}$, so $\angle V$ must be less than $122^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: In any triangle, all three angles sum to 180°. With ∠U = 58°: ∠V + ∠W = 180° − 58° = 122°.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: For a valid triangle, every angle must be greater than 0°. So ∠W = 122° − ∠V must be positive, which means ∠V < 122°.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Check each option: A) 121° $\Rightarrow$ ∠W = 1° > 0 (valid). B) 123° $\Rightarrow$ ∠W = −1° (impossible). C) 131° $\Rightarrow$ ∠W = −9° (impossible). D) 180° $\Rightarrow$ ∠W = −58° (impossible). Only A works. That is the correct answer.

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — $121$: ∠W = 122° − 121° = 1° > 0°. All three angles (58°, 121°, 1°) are positive and sum to 180°. Valid triangle. ✓
  • Choice B — $123$: ∠W = 122° − 123° = −1°. A negative angle is impossible in a triangle. ✗
  • Choice C — $131$: ∠W = 122° − 131° = −9°. A negative angle is impossible in a triangle. ✗
  • Choice D — $180$: ∠V alone equals 180°, which would account for the entire angle sum by itself, leaving no room for ∠U or ∠W. Impossible. ✗
That is the correct answer.
